text:
The rain poured hard down the narrow streets of Whiterun, the grey clouds hanging low like heavy blankets. At the sound of the patter of raindrops on glass, a young thief named Faelon decided he couldn't brave the cold for one more night sneaking through the market to steal that pricey rug its previous owner had fallen on hard times over.

The wind howled, and a rogue gust nearly knocked him off his feet. He quickened his steps, the wet carpet clinging to his shoes. The shifty merchant looked suspiciously up from his mug. Faelon smiled charmingly and offered a counterfeit coin. The merchant's lips twisted into a sneer before he pocketed the fraudulent goodliness, satisfied with a night of easy coin and a few minutes' peace.

He tucked himself into the nearest alley, pulling his cloak tighter around him. As he pulled on his stolen boots, he swore he caught a flicker of the wary gaze of the merchant. No chance. A
